steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 1 calls root-to-root separation "an essential step" and Text 2 calls it "the necessary precaution." Despite disagreeing on whether Wang's full design succeeds, both authors treat separating root systems as a required foundation for testing CMN nutrient transfer.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — makes a claim unsupported by either text: invents a point neither author raises. ✗
  • Choice B — confuses necessity with sufficiency: both authors say the step is necessary, not that it alone guarantees a valid result. ✗
  • Choice C — without precluding root-to-root transfer, determining the CMN mechanism is impossible: states exactly the prerequisite both texts share. ✓
  • Choice D — imposes an unstated requirement: adds a condition neither author mentions. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
